jsx-tag.comment:
Tags with attributes: or
children
jsx-tag.name: meta.tag.metascript

jsx-tag-attribute-name.comment: Attribute name with optional namespace: ns:name

import-export.comment: Import/export declarations — delegates to structured import-declaration and export-declaration

control-statement.comment: Control flow keywords — split by category per TS reference

for-loop.comment: for loop — handles for (;;), for..of, for..in, for await..of

match-expression.comment: MetaScript match expression/statement: match (expr) { pattern => result, ... }
match-expression.name: meta.match.metascript

extern-declaration.comment: extern function/const/var/class/enum with optional from binding

try-expression.comment: try operator: try expr catch fallback

patterns.comment: try keyword followed by expression (not try { block })
catch in try-expression context (not try/catch block)

keywords.comment: Remaining keywords not handled by structured rules

types.comment: Top-level type patterns — only primitives and built-ins. type-annotation is context-specific (used inside declarations only).

type-annotation.comment: Type annotation after colon: x: Type, param: Type

type.comment: Master type rule — includes all type constructs

type-builtin.comment: Built-in generic types (highlighted everywhere, not just type context)
type-builtin.name: support.type.builtin.metascript

type-builtin-literals.comment: Literal types in type context: true, false, null, undefined

type-parameters.comment: Generic parameter declarations: <T, K extends string>
type-parameters.name: meta.type.parameters.metascript

type-object.comment: Object types: { x: number; y: string }
type-object.name: meta.object.type.metascript

type-operators.comment: Type operators: |, &, keyof, typeof, readonly, infer

type-paren-or-function-type.comment: Parenthesized or function types: (T) or (x: T) => U

patterns.comment: Parameter name with type: name: Type

arrow-function.comment: Arrow functions: async? (params) => body or async? param => body

patterns.comment: Arrow function with return type: (params): Type => body

destructuring-variable.comment: Destructuring: const { a, b } = obj; or const [x, y] = arr;

method-declaration.comment: Method in class body: [modifiers] name(params): Type { }

field-declaration.comment: Field in class/interface body: [modifiers] name?: Type = value;

support-objects.comment: Built-in global objects and their methods

parameter-name.comment: Parameter name with optional modifiers: [public|private|readonly] [...] name[?]

return-type.comment: Return type annotation after closing paren: ): ReturnType

var-expr.comment: Variable declarations: const/let/var name: Type = value

var-single-const.comment: Single const variable name (scoped as constant)

class-declaration.comment: Class declaration: [export] [abstract] class Name<T> extends Base implements I { }
class-declaration.name: meta.class.metascript

class-heritage.comment: extends/implements clauses (legacy, delegates to structured rule)

decl-block.comment: Declaration body block { ... } — includes method/field declarations for class/interface bodies

type-arguments.comment: Type arguments at call sites: foo<string>()
type-arguments.name: meta.type.parameters.metascript

type-conditional.comment: Conditional type: T extends U ? A : B

function-expression.comment: Anonymous function expression: const f = function() {}
function-expression.name: meta.function.expression.metascript

template.comment: Template literals with tagged template support

parameter-type-annotation.comment: Tighter type annotation for parameters — ends at , or )

function-call.comment: Function call with optional chaining and type arguments

expression-operators.comment: Expression keyword operators: typeof, void, delete, instanceof, in, of

destructuring-const.comment: Group A: Destructuring for const declarations — names scoped as constants

destructuring-variable-rest.comment: Group A: ...name in destructuring, scoped as readwrite variable

destructuring-variable-rest-const.comment: Group A: ...name in const destructuring, scoped as constant

object-binding-pattern-const.comment: Group A: { a, b } in const destructuring — names scoped as constants

array-binding-pattern-const.comment: Group A: [a, b] in const destructuring — names scoped as constants

object-binding-element.comment: Group A: Element inside { } destructuring pattern (readwrite)

object-binding-element-const.comment: Group A: Element inside { } const destructuring pattern (constant)

object-binding-element-propertyName.comment: Group A: The property name before : in { prop: localName }

binding-element.comment: Group A: Binding element in destructuring (readwrite context)

binding-element-const.comment: Group A: Binding element in const destructuring (constant context)

destructuring-parameter.comment: Group A: Destructuring in function parameters

parameter-object-binding-element.comment: Group A: Element inside { } destructuring in parameters

parameter-object-binding-pattern.comment: Group A: { a, b } pattern nested in parameter destructuring

parameter-array-binding-pattern.comment: Group A: [a, b] pattern nested in parameter destructuring

parameter-binding-element.comment: Group A: Sub-patterns for destructured parameter elements

object-member.comment: Group B: Master rule for object literal members

patterns.comment: Property name followed by function/arrow (method shorthand via colon)
patterns.name: meta.object.member.metascript

patterns.comment: Shorthand property: { name } where name === variable
patterns.name: meta.object.member.metascript

object-literal-method-declaration.comment: Group B: { methodName() { } } or { async get name() {} }
object-literal-method-declaration.name: meta.method.declaration.metascript

object-identifiers.comment: Group B: Shorthand property { name } where name is a variable

patterns.comment: Object property access chain with constant identifier

import-declaration.comment: Group C: import { X } from 'Y' / import X from 'Y' / import * as X from 'Y'
import-declaration.name: meta.import.metascript

export-declaration.comment: Group C: export { X } / export default / export * from

import-export-declaration.comment: Group C: Shared patterns for import/export declarations

import-export-block.comment: Group C: The { name1, name2 as alias } part
import-export-block.name: meta.block.metascript

import-export-clause.comment: Group C: name, name as alias, * as alias, type name, default

patterns.comment: name as alias or * as alias or default as alias

class-expression.comment: Group D: const C = class Name { } anonymous/named class expression
class-expression.name: meta.class.metascript

class-declaration-or-expression-patterns.comment: Group D: Shared patterns for class declarations and expressions

class-or-interface-heritage.comment: Group D: extends Base<T>, implements I1, I2 with type arguments

class-or-interface-body.comment: Group D: Specialized body for class/interface with method/field declarations

patterns.comment: Expression after colon (to avoid false field matches)

access-modifier.comment: Group D: Access modifiers for class members
access-modifier.name: storage.modifier.metascript

async-modifier.comment: Group D: async modifier for class methods
async-modifier.name: storage.modifier.async.metascript

arrow-return-type.comment: Group E: ): Type after arrow function params
arrow-return-type.name: meta.return.type.arrow.metascript

arrow-return-type-body.comment: Group E: The type content after the colon in arrow return type

patterns.comment: Handle object type after colon so it is not confused with function block

decorator.comment: Group F: Structured decorator: @ followed by expression (handles @foo.bar())
decorator.name: meta.decorator.metascript

template-call.comment: Group F: Tagged template literals with type arguments: tag<T>`string`

patterns.comment: Tagged template with optional type arguments

type-paren-or-function-parameters.comment: Group F: Parenthesized function params in type context (more precise)
type-paren-or-function-parameters.name: meta.type.paren.cover.metascript

patterns.comment: Parameter name with type annotation (function-like context)
